data_description.name: Display name shown in the Home Assistant UI for this integration instance.
data_description.wifi_interface: The network interface to scan for WiFi networks (e.g., wlan0). Available interfaces are detected automatically where possible.
data_description.known_wifi_ids: Comma-separated list of SSIDs to treat as known (e.g., Home-WiFi, Guest-Network). Wildcards supported (e.g., Guest_*). Networks not matching this list are counted as unknown.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate WiFi SSID Monitor
reauth_confirm.description: The Supervisor API token is invalid or has expired. Please confirm to re-validate.

data_description.name: Display name shown in the Home Assistant UI for this integration instance.
data_description.wifi_interface: The network interface to scan for WiFi networks (e.g., wlan0). Available interfaces are detected automatically where possible.
data_description.known_wifi_ids: Comma-separated list of SSIDs to treat as known (e.g., Home-WiFi, Guest-Network). Wildcards supported (e.g., Guest_*). Networks not matching this list are counted as unknown.
data_description.denylist_ssids: Comma-separated list of SSIDs or patterns that are always counted as unknown, even if they also appear in the known list. Wildcards supported (e.g., Guest_*). This list takes priority over the known list.
data_description.last_seen_ttl_days: How many days to retain last-seen history for networks that are no longer visible. Set to 0 to keep history forever. Range: 0–366 days. Default is 90 days. Scan interval, band filters, hidden-network inclusion and the proximity threshold are now separate control entities on the device page — not set here.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to Supervisor API.

scan_failed.message: WiFi scan failed — check Home Assistant Repairs for details

entry_not_found.message: No wifi_ssid_monitor entry found with ID {entry_id}

supervisor_unavailable.title: WiFi SSID Monitor cannot reach the Supervisor API
supervisor_unavailable.description: The integration has failed to connect to the Home Assistant Supervisor API for multiple consecutive scan cycles. WiFi scan data is now stale. Check that the Supervisor service is running and that the monitored interface is valid under **Settings > System > Network**.

interface_missing.title: WiFi SSID Monitor interface not found
interface_missing.description:
The configured WiFi interface is no longer reported by the Supervisor. It may have been renamed or removed. Check **Settings > System > Network**, then reconfigure the integration to select the correct interface.

Detail: {detail}

signal_format_changed.title: WiFi SSID Monitor signal format changed
signal_format_changed.description:
The Supervisor changed the unit it reports signal strength in (percentage vs dBm). Signal values have been converted automatically, but review the Proximity Signal Threshold to make sure the alert still fires when you expect.

Detail: {detail}

data_description.known_wifi_ids: Comma-separated list of SSIDs to treat as known (e.g., Home-WiFi, Guest-Network). Wildcards supported (e.g., Guest_*). Networks not matching this list are counted as unknown.
data_description.denylist_ssids: Comma-separated list of SSIDs or patterns that are always counted as unknown, even if they also appear in the known list. Wildcards supported (e.g., Guest_*). This list takes priority over the known list.
data_description.last_seen_ttl_days: How many days to retain last-seen history for networks that are no longer visible. Set to 0 to keep history forever. Range: 0–366 days. Default is 90 days. Scan interval, band filters, hidden-network inclusion and the proximity threshold are now separate control entities on the device page — not set here.
